text: A schematic view shows the port placement for reduced port robotic rectal surgery. A single 3.5 cm vertical incision is made through the umbilical skin, and the glove port is inserted into the intra-abdominal space. After pneumoperitoneum is achieved, an additional single port for wristed robotic instruments and rectal retraction is inserted in the right lower quadrant. The operative view displays the access port setup for the procedure.

The surgeon performs a totally robotic approach using dual docking technique. Lymph nodes around the root of the inferior mesenteric artery are dissected, and the artery is ligated with a hemlock clip plier through the artery arm port. During medial to lateral dissection, the gonadal vessels and ureter are identified, and the left pericolic gutter is dissected up to the level of the spleen.

The robotic cart is repositioned between the patient's legs for the pelvic stage of the procedure. The assistant surgeon uses an additional single port to retract the recto-sigmoid cephalad. Two 300mm curved cannulas are used for deep pelvic dissection, following total mesorectum excision principles and autonomic nerve preservation. Posterior pelvic dissection is performed along the rectal proper fascia, enveloping the rectum and mesorectum, while preserving the inferior hypogastric nerves. The lateral mesorectal fascia is dissected off from the pelvic plexus, with dense adhesions meticulously separated. The left middle rectal artery is controlled with monopolar diathermy. Anterolateral dissection continues until reaching the previously dissected posterolateral area at the lateral wall, extending posterior to the vagina. The magnified robotic view aids in determining the correct surgical plane.

The surgeon uses three 45mm endo-wrist staplers through the additional single port in the right lower quadrant for distal bowel resection. The tumor specimen is extracted through the transumbilical incision. An end-to-end intracorporeal anastomosis is performed using a double stapling technique, and a suction drain is placed in the pelvic cavity through the additional single port site.
